name: KrillClaw
description: The world's smallest AI agent runtime. 49KB. Written in Zig. Zero dependencies.

commit.message:
chore: move site to private repo for competitive security

Site content (positioning, competitor comparisons, messaging) is now
in the private krillclaw/krillclaw-site repo. Deploys to krillclaw.com
from there via Cloudflare Pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(site): restore hero mascot, add use cases, update Phase 5 numbers

- Replace shrimp emoji hero with pixel art krill mascot image
- Add "What becomes possible" use case stories (smart home, agriculture,
  industrial) near the top of the page
- Update all binary sizes: 49KB вҶ’ <59KB lite, вүӨ500KB вҶ’ 407KB full
- Update test count: 60 вҶ’ 125+
- Update LOC: ~4,800 вҶ’ ~6,800, file count: 16 вҶ’ 23
- Update FAQ, meta tags, OG tags, JSON-LD structured data
- Add OG image meta tag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 5 вҖ” Lite binary, tool annotations, multilingual stop, edge intelligence

Complete Phase 5 technical track:

- A3: Tool annotations on all 28 tools (readOnly/destructive/sideEffects)
- A4: Multilingual stop phrases (26 phrases, 10 languages, FNV-1a comptime hash)
- A5: Lite binary with embedded build path (-Dembedded=true):
  - Split-brain agent: BLE transport, phone proxies LLM API calls
  - api_ble.zig + api_parse.zig (shared response parsing)
  - FixedArena allocator (128KB, no GPA)
  - Trigger engine: local threshold rules, 6 operators, 4 actions
  - Sensor module: ring buffer, Welford's stats, Z-score anomaly, named registry
  - Fault log: boot breadcrumbs for crash diagnosis
  - Event queue: offline circular buffer, JSON drain on BLE reconnect
  - Power budget: per-subsystem ВөWh tracking, battery life estimation
  - Swarm primitives: agent identity, capability manifest, task protocol,
    status beacon, result envelope
  - Adaptive cron backoff: interval doubles on idle, resets on rule fire
  - BLE push wake infrastructure
  - 65+ new tests across 8 new modules

Also includes Phase 4 bridge improvements: Discord/Slack/WhatsApp channel
fixes, MCP bridge enhancements, hardware tool updates, Python test suite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: remove internal strategy docs and sensitive files from repo

- Remove HANDOFF.md, CLAUDE.md, PHASE4-PLAN.md, PHASE5-PLAN.md,
  COMPETITIVE-TABLE.md from git tracking (kept locally)
- Expand .gitignore: .claude/, *.key, *.pem, internal docs patterns
- Fix stale TinyDancer reference in smoke-test.sh

Files remain on disk but are no longer pushed to the public repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 4 вҖ” MCP client, 3 channels, GPIO, terminal demos, KPI updates

MCP client support:
- mcp_bridge.py: connects to N MCP servers via official SDK
- Tools namespaced as servername__toolname, supports stdio + HTTP
- Config from ~/.krillclaw/mcp_servers.json (Claude Desktop format)
- Integrated into bridge.py --serve and --exec-tool modes

New channels (4вҶ’7):
- Discord (discord.py), Slack (slack-bolt Socket Mode), WhatsApp (Cloud API)
- All registered in bridge.py channel registry with lazy imports

GPIO/hardware tools:
- hardware.py: gpio_read, gpio_write, gpio_list, i2c_read, spi_transfer
- Linux via libgpiod, macOS simulator mode
- Pin allowlist + rate limiting for safety
- Zig IoT profile tool definitions (+200 bytes)

Website updates:
- Interactive terminal demos (before/after car ECU + elevator)
- KPIs: 20+ providers, 7 channels, 60 tests, 4800 LOC
- MCP Compatible + 7 Channels feature cards
- Comparison table: MCP + GPIO rows added

Provider docs: added Perplexity, Cohere, AI21, Hyperbolic, Lepton AI
README: MCP, Channels, GPIO sections added

Binary: 463KB (aarch64-linux) вҖ” GPIO tools add ~200 bytes to IoT profile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: retrigger deploy after adding Cloudflare secrets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: touch site file to trigger deploy workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move Cloudflare env vars to job level for wrangler v3

Step-level env vars don't propagate to wrangler's internal subprocess.
Moving CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID to job-level env
ensures wrangler can read them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: trigger deploy workflow to verify Cloudflare fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update integration test binary size gate to 600KB

The integration.sh size gate was still at 300KB (Phase 1 value).
Aligns with the test.yml CI gate updated in the Phase 3 commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 3 вҖ” WebSocket gateway, multi-channel bridge, skills/plugins

CI fixes: Zig 0.13вҶ’0.15.2 in test.yml, size gate 300KBвҶ’600KB, wrangler
env token in deploy.yml.

Multi-channel bridge (--serve mode): Channel ABC with Telegram, MQTT,
webhook, and WebSocket implementations. MessageRouter dispatches
incoming messages through the agent and routes responses back.

WebSocket gateway: subprocess-per-message agent execution with streaming
stdout as WebSocket frames. Wire protocol: messageвҶ’textвҶ’done.

Skills/plugin framework: ~/.krillclaw/plugins/*.py auto-discovered on
bridge startup. Unknown tools in Zig fall through to bridge for plugin
dispatch (~3 lines of Zig, everything else in Python).

Binary cost: 0 bytes (525KB unchanged from Phase 2 baseline).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 0-2 вҖ” security fixes, rename, cron scheduler, shared tools, OTA, web search, session persistence

Phase 0 вҖ” Security & Rename:
- Fix transport.zig JSON injection (P0 from audit)
- Complete YoctoClaw вҶ’ KrillClaw rename across all files
- Add regression tests for security fixes

Phase 1 вҖ” Provider docs, cross-arch validation, website:
- Add Docs/PROVIDERS.md (16 confirmed OpenAI-compatible providers)
- Expand cross-architecture validation to 44 configurations (10 Linux + 13 bare-metal)
- Add Docs/CROSS-CHECK-RESULTS.md with full test matrix
- Fix website copy: "350+ devices" вҶ’ "50+ devices", "17+" вҶ’ "16" providers
- Add CLAUDE.md project instructions

Phase 2 вҖ” Features:
- Add cron/heartbeat scheduler (src/cron.zig) for daemon mode on edge devices
- Add shared tools architecture (src/tools_shared.zig) вҖ” get_current_time, kv_get/set/list/delete
- Add bridge-delegated tools: web_search, session_save/load/list, ota_check/update
- Implement OTA update system in bridge.py (GitHub releases check/download/apply)
- Implement web search via DuckDuckGo HTML (no API keys)
- Implement session persistence (JSON file storage)
- Fix ASCII banner (YoctoClaw вҶ’ KrillClaw)
- Remove duplicate kv tools from tools_iot.zig (now in shared)

Binary size: coding/aarch64-linux 452KB (+13KB for all Phase 2 features)
QA: 39 unit tests pass, 15 cross-compile targets pass, 6 Docker execution tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Update README.md
fix: correct build.zig.zon name syntax for Zig 0.13 + rename yoctoclaw to krillclaw
Remove old v2 homepage
Clean .gitignore
polish: rewrite README, expand CONTRIBUTING, add hardware report template, reformat CHANGELOG

chore: normalize naming conventions

Complete brand rename across source code, docs, marketing materials,
store templates, and review files. Zero KrillClaw refs remaining.
ci: add Cloudflare Pages auto-deploy on site changes
feat: add GitHub + Get Started nav bar to site
BSL 1.1: 3-year conversion, generous use grant (<M rev or <10K devices)
fix: SEO/GEO improvements вҖ” H1, schema, alt text, robots.txt
test: alternative landing page v2 at /v2

chore: add OSS infrastructure, fix LICENSE to BSL 1.1

- LICENSE: MIT вҶ’ BSL 1.1 (matches project decision)
- Added SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
- Added issue/PR templates
- Updated CONTRIBUTING.md
- Removed PAT from git remote URL

fix: Zig 0.15 compatibility and test fixes

- Fix ArrayList.writer() calls to pass allocator (Zig 0.15 API change)
- Make json.writeEscaped pub for cross-module access
- Fix macOS /private/tmp symlink in path allowlist (isCanonicalPathAllowed)
- Remove overly restrictive parent dir check in isPathAllowed
- Fix memory leaks in executeBash (free unescaped_cmd, unused stdout/stderr)
- Use page_allocator in tool tests (tools use arena pattern, not per-alloc free)
- Update injection test assertions to accept "not allowed" response
- All 40/40 tests passing, 0 leaks

site: add fixed website HTML for A+ grade

- Fix license contradiction: /terms now references BSL 1.1 (was MIT)
- Add skip-to-content link for keyboard users
- Add ARIA landmarks (header, main, footer roles)
- Add aria-label on marquee sections, aria-hidden on duplicate rows
- Fix empty alt tags on mascot images (descriptive alt text)
- Add OG image dimensions (1200x630)
- Add hreflang tags (en + zh)
- Fix footer links: privacy.html/terms.html -> /privacy /terms
- Fix git clone URL: yoctoclaw -> krillclaw org
- Fix privacy page: GitHub Pages -> Cloudflare hosting reference
- Replace generic chip icons with SVG ESP32 chip illustrations
- Add breadcrumb structured data on subpages
- Add author/url to SoftwareApplication schema
- Add meta descriptions and OG tags to terms/privacy pages
- Add email fallback text (Cloudflare obfuscation workaround)

Merge feature/emulator-tests: Zig 0.15 compatible build system

- Fix build.zig.zon for Zig 0.15 (enum literal .name, add .fingerprint)
- Add ReAct agent loop
- Security fixes (auth_buf, TOCTOU, memory leaks)
- Comprehensive test suites
License: BSL 1.1 with Apache 2.0 conversion
Initial commit: KrillClaw - The world's smallest AI agent runtime

readme_html:
~450KB binary. 0 dependencies. 19 source files. 50+ validated device targets. Runs on a $3 microcontroller or a cloud server.

KrillClaw is an autonomous AI agent runtime written in Zig. It connects to 20+ LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Ollama + 17 via --base-url), executes tools, and loops until the task is done. Includes cron scheduling, persistent KV store, MCP support, 7 messaging channels, GPIO/hardware control, and BLE/Serial transports for edge devices.

 в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
 в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ   ~450 KB.  Zero deps.  Boots in <10ms.              в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ   The entire agent runtime вҖ” LLM client, tool        в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ   executor, JSON parser, SSE streaming, cron,        в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ   KV store, context mgmt вҖ” in 4,576 lines of Zig.   в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Quick Start

# 1. Install Zig 0.15+ вҶ’ https://ziglang.org/download/
# 2. Clone and build (takes ~1 second)
git clone https://github.com/krillclaw/KrillClaw.git
cd KrillClaw
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# 3. Set your API key and go
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
./zig-out/bin/krillclaw "create a REST API in Go with user auth"

That's it. No npm install. No pip. No Docker. Just Zig and a binary.

Why KrillClaw?

Every AI agent runtime is massive. Desktop coding agents ship as 50вҖ“500MB bundles with hundreds of dependencies. The actual logic вҖ” "call LLM, parse response, execute tools, repeat" вҖ” shouldn't need any of that.

KrillClaw proves it doesn't. The same agentic loop that powers desktop tools, compiled to a binary smaller than a JPEG, running on hardware that costs less than a coffee.

KrillClaw exists because AI agents should run everywhere вҖ” not just on machines with Node.js and 8GB of RAM.

The Numbers

KrillClaw Typical Edge Runtime Desktop Agent
Binary ~450 KB 2вҖ“8 MB 50вҖ“500 MB
RAM ~2 MB 10вҖ“512 MB 150 MB вҖ“ 1 GB
Source 4,576 LOC 5вҖ“30K LOC 30вҖ“100K+ LOC
Dependencies 0 10вҖ“100+ 100вҖ“1000+
Boot time <10 ms <1s 2вҖ“5s
Embedded/BLE Yes Sometimes No
Cron/Daemon Yes Sometimes No

vs Embedded/Edge Runtimes

Feature KrillClaw MimiClaw PicoClaw
Language Zig Python Go
Binary size ~450 KB ~2 MB ~8 MB
RAM usage ~2 MB ~512 KB* ~10 MB
Dependencies 0 pip Go modules
BLE transport вң… вқҢ вқҢ
Serial transport вң… вқҢ вқҢ
Multi-provider 20+ (Claude, OpenAI, Ollama + 17 via --base-url) 2 1
SSE streaming вң… вқҢ вқҢ
Inline tests 60 0 Limited
Sandbox mode вң… вқҢ вқҢ
MCP support вң… вқҢ вқҢ
Channels 7 (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, MQTT, WebSocket, Webhook) 2 N/A
GPIO / hardware вң… (GPIO, I2C, SPI) вң… (GPIO) вқҢ
License BSL 1.1 MIT MIT

Competitor data as of Feb 2026. Check their repos for current numbers.

Architecture

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

19 files. 4,576 lines. Zero dependencies.

Source Map

File Lines Role
main.zig 198 CLI, REPL, cron daemon entry point
agent.zig 215 Agent loop + FNV-1a stuck-loop detection
api.zig 341 Multi-provider HTTP client (Claude/OpenAI/Ollama)
stream.zig 362 SSE streaming parser
json.zig 519 Hand-rolled JSON parser/builder вҖ” zero deps
tools.zig 191 Tool dispatcher вҖ” comptime profile + shared tools
tools_shared.zig 362 Shared tools: time, KV, web search, sessions, OTA (all profiles)
tools_coding.zig 484 Coding profile: bash, read/write/edit, search, list, patch
tools_iot.zig 176 IoT profile: MQTT, HTTP, device info
tools_robotics.zig 153 Robotics profile: commands, e-stop, telemetry
context.zig 226 Token estimation + priority-based truncation
config.zig 202 Config: file вҶ’ env вҶ’ CLI precedence
cron.zig 206 Cron/heartbeat scheduler for daemon mode
transport.zig 179 Abstract vtable transport + RPC protocol
types.zig 157 Core types: Provider, Message, Config, ToolDef
ble.zig 159 BLE GATT transport (protocol + simulation)
serial.zig 142 UART/serial transport (Linux/macOS)
arena.zig 200 Fixed arena allocator for embedded targets
react.zig 104 ReAct reasoning loop

Profiles

Compile-time profiles select different tool sets. Only the selected profile's code ships in the binary вҖ” zero runtime overhead.

# Coding agent (default)
zig build -Dprofile=coding -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# IoT agent вҖ” MQTT, HTTP, KV store, device info
zig build -Dprofile=iot -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# Robotics agent вҖ” motion commands, e-stop, telemetry
zig build -Dprofile=robotics -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall
Profile Tools Binary Size Security Policy
coding bash, read/write/edit, search, list, patch + shared ~459 KB bash behind approval gate, writes restricted to cwd
iot MQTT pub/sub, HTTP, GPIO, I2C, SPI, device info + shared ~463 KB no bash, no file writes, 30 req/min rate limit
robotics robot_cmd, estop, telemetry + shared ~473 KB no bash, bounds checking, 10 cmd/s, e-stop

All profiles include shared tools available across every profile:

  • get_current_time вҖ” ISO-8601 timestamp
  • kv_get / kv_set / kv_list / kv_delete вҖ” persistent key-value store

All profiles support sandbox mode: zig build -Dsandbox=true

Cron / Heartbeat (Daemon Mode)

Run KrillClaw as a scheduled agent on edge devices:

# Run agent every 5 minutes with a custom prompt
krillclaw --cron-interval 300 --cron-prompt "check sensors and report anomalies"

# Heartbeat every 60 seconds + agent every 10 minutes
krillclaw --heartbeat 60 --cron-interval 600

# Run 10 times then exit
krillclaw --cron-interval 120 --cron-max-runs 10 --cron-prompt "collect data"

Designed for edge devices running data collection cycles between connectivity windows (BLE, Serial). The scheduler has minimal binary cost (~2KB) and uses no threads.

Providers

KrillClaw supports 20+ LLM providers through three protocol backends. Any provider with an OpenAI-compatible API works via --base-url.

Provider Models Auth
Claude claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-opus-4, etc. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
OpenAI gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo, etc. OPENAI_API_KEY
Ollama llama3, codellama, mistral, etc. None (local)
+ 17 more via --base-url Provider-specific 
# Claude (default)
./zig-out/bin/krillclaw "fix the tests"

# OpenAI
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
./zig-out/bin/krillclaw --provider openai -m gpt-4o "fix the tests"

# Local Ollama
./zig-out/bin/krillclaw --provider ollama -m llama3 "explain this code"

# Groq (ultra-fast inference)
KRILLCLAW_API_KEY=gsk_... ./zig-out/bin/krillclaw \
  --provider openai --base-url https://api.groq.com/openai \
  -m llama-3.3-70b-versatile "optimize this function"

# DeepSeek (cost-effective coding)
KRILLCLAW_API_KEY=sk-... ./zig-out/bin/krillclaw \
  --provider openai --base-url https://api.deepseek.com \
  -m deepseek-chat "refactor this module"

# Together AI (open-source models)
KRILLCLAW_API_KEY=... ./zig-out/bin/krillclaw \
  --provider openai --base-url https://api.together.xyz \
  -m meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct-Turbo "write tests"

# Google Gemini (via OpenAI compatibility layer)
KRILLCLAW_API_KEY=... ./zig-out/bin/krillclaw \
  --provider openai \
  --base-url https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai \
  -m gemini-2.0-flash "summarize this repo"

See Docs/PROVIDERS.md for the full list of 20+ supported providers with base URLs, tool calling support, and configuration examples.

MCP Support

KrillClaw integrates with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, connecting your agent to 1000+ tools.

# Configure MCP servers in ~/.krillclaw/mcp_servers.json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/home/user"]
    }
  }
}

# Start with MCP tools available
python bridge/bridge.py --serve --channels webhook

MCP tools are namespaced as servername__toolname and automatically available to the agent. Supports stdio and streamable HTTP transports.

Channels

7 messaging channels вҖ” your agent talks wherever your users are:

Channel Library Auth
Telegram stdlib (urllib) Bot token
Discord discord.py Bot token
Slack slack-bolt Bot + App token (Socket Mode)
WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) Business access token
MQTT paho-mqtt Broker config
WebSocket websockets Token auth
Webhook stdlib (http.server) Bearer token 
# Start multi-channel server
python bridge/bridge.py --serve --channels telegram,discord,webhook

GPIO / Hardware Control

Direct hardware control from AI вҖ” GPIO, I2C, SPI:

# IoT profile includes GPIO tools
zig build -Dprofile=iot -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# GPIO tools route through the Python bridge
# Linux: real hardware via libgpiod
# macOS: simulator mode (logs commands)
Tool Description
gpio_read Read a GPIO pin value
gpio_write Write a value to a GPIO pin
gpio_list List available GPIO pins
i2c_read Read from an I2C device
spi_transfer Transfer data over SPI

Safety: pin allowlist via ~/.krillclaw/hardware.json, rate limiting on writes.

Embedded Mode

KrillClaw targets microcontrollers. The device runs the agent brain. A phone or laptop bridges to the internet.

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       BLE/UART       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      HTTPS      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  KrillClaw  в”Ӯ в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә в”Ӯ    Bridge     в”Ӯ в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә в”Ӯ  LLM    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  (device)   в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ  (phone/PC)   в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  API    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ  "call bash ls"      в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в”Ӯ Agent loop  в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә    в”Ӯ Execute tools в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ JSON parse  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ Return result в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ State mgmt  в”Ӯ в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    ~50 KB   в”Ӯ  "file1 file2..."    в”Ӯ  bridge.py    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Build for Hardware

# BLE transport
zig build -Dble=true -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# Serial/UART transport
zig build -Dserial=true -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

# Bare-metal (no OS)
zig build -Dembedded=true -Dtarget=thumb-none-eabi -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

Target Hardware

Device SoC RAM Flash Cost
ESP32-C3 RISC-V 400 KB 4 MB $3
Raspberry Pi Pico W RP2040 264 KB 2 MB $6
Colmi R02 (smart ring) BlueX RF03 ~32 KB ~256 KB $20
nRF52840-DK nRF52840 256 KB 1 MB $40
nRF5340-DK nRF5340 512 KB 1 MB $50

Fixed Arena Allocator

For devices with no OS heap:

var mem = arena.Arena32K.init();  // 32KB вҖ” fits on nRF5340
const alloc = mem.allocator();
// ... use alloc for everything ...
mem.reset();  // Frees everything at once between agent turns

Preset sizes: Arena4K, Arena16K, Arena32K, Arena128K, Arena256K.

Transport Layers

Transport Use Case Status
HTTP Desktop вҖ” direct HTTPS to API Stable
BLE Embedded вҖ” GATT protocol + desktop simulation via Unix socket Experimental
Serial Dev boards вҖ” UART to host machine Experimental

BLE note: The BLE transport implements framing and GATT service UUIDs with desktop simulation via Unix sockets. Real hardware integration requires linking against the platform BLE SDK (e.g., Nordic SoftDevice). See ble.zig for integration points.

Multi-Channel Gateway (Phase 3)

The bridge supports multiple message channels simultaneously via --serve mode:

# Start with webhook channel (default)
python bridge/bridge/bridge.py --serve --channels webhook

# Start with multiple channels
python bridge/bridge/bridge.py --serve --channels telegram,webhook,websocket

# WebSocket gateway for browser-based clients
python bridge/bridge/bridge.py --serve --channels websocket

Available Channels

Channel Transport Use Case
webhook HTTP POST Simplest integration вҖ” any HTTP client
websocket WebSocket Browser clients, streaming responses
telegram Telegram Bot API Chat-based interaction
mqtt MQTT pub/sub IoT device messaging

WebSocket Wire Protocol

вҶ’ {"type": "message", "text": "fix the bug"}
вҶҗ {"type": "text", "text": "Let me look at that..."}
вҶҗ {"type": "done"}

Channel Configuration

Per-channel settings via ~/.krillclaw/channels.json:

{
  "webhook": {"port": 8080, "auth_token": "secret"},
  "websocket": {"port": 8765, "agent_binary": "./zig-out/bin/krillclaw"},
  "telegram": {"token": "bot123:ABC", "allowed_users": [12345]},
  "mqtt": {"broker": "localhost", "subscribe_topic": "krillclaw/in"}
}

Skills / Plugins

Extend the agent with custom Python tools. Drop a .py file in ~/.krillclaw/plugins/:

# ~/.krillclaw/plugins/my_tool.py
TOOL_NAME = "my_custom_tool"
TOOL_DESCRIPTION = "Does something custom."
TOOL_SCHEMA = {"type": "object", "properties": {"input": {"type": "string"}}}

def handle(data: dict) -> dict:
    return {"result": f"processed: {data.get('input', '')}"}

Plugins are discovered on bridge startup. Unknown tools from the Zig agent automatically fall through to the bridge, which routes them to the matching plugin handler. Built-in tool names cannot be overridden.

Configuration

# Environment variables
export KRILLCLAW_MODEL=claude-opus-4-6
export KRILLCLAW_PROVIDER=claude
export KRILLCLAW_BASE_URL=https://my-proxy.com
export KRILLCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT="You are a Go expert..."

// .krillclaw.json (project-level config)
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
  "provider": "claude",
  "max_tokens": 8192,
  "streaming": true
}

Config precedence: CLI flags вҶ’ environment variables вҶ’ config file.

REPL Commands

Command Description
/help Show help
/quit /exit /q Exit
/model <name> Switch model
/provider <name> Switch provider

Design Decisions

Decision Rationale
Hand-rolled JSON std.json adds unnecessary code. KrillClaw only needs key extraction + body building. 501 lines, zero deps.
Vtable transports Same binary works over HTTP, BLE, or Serial. Swap physical layer without touching agent logic.
FNV-1a loop detection Detect stuck LLM loops in constant memory (128 bytes). Critical for embedded.
Priority-based truncation When context fills, drop assistant text first, keep tool results. Keeps working memory functional.
Substring search, not regex Regex engines are ~10K+ lines. std.mem.indexOf covers 90%+ of agent search use cases.

Testing

zig build test        # 39 inline unit tests
bash test/integration.sh  # 9 integration tests

Tests cover JSON parsing, SSE streaming, arena allocation, context truncation, tool execution, glob matching, and security injection attempts.

CI runs on every push with a binary size gate (<600KB).

Building

zig build                              # Debug build
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall      # Smallest binary
zig build test                         # Run all tests
zig build size                         # Report binary size

Security

KrillClaw executes tools with the permissions of the running user. Do not run with elevated privileges. Use profiles and sandbox mode to restrict tool access.

BLE and Serial transports do not currently include encryption or authentication. Use only on trusted networks.

See SECURITY.md for reporting vulnerabilities.

Known Limitations

  • JSON parser is flat вҖ” finds first matching key at any depth (works for LLM API responses where keys are unambiguous)
  • Token estimation is heuristic вҖ” ~4 chars/token approximation, not billing-accurate
  • Session persistence via bridge вҖ” save/load conversation history through bridge tools
  • BLE transport is protocol-only вҖ” real hardware needs platform BLE SDK linking
  • Serial baud uses stty вҖ” Linux/macOS only
  • Requires Zig 0.15+
